message:
fix(codec/proptest): emit serial before ident in structured_header_frame to match wire format

The `structured_header_frame` helper introduced in f43cc0ee had the two
leb128 header fields in the wrong order relative to the real PDU frame
format documented in frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs. The codec's decode_raw
reads fields in this sequence:

  tagged_len (leb128 u64)
  serial     (leb128 u64)
  ident      (leb128 u64)
  data       (tagged_len - len(serial) - len(ident) bytes)

The helper was doing:

  len_word = leb128(len)
  buf.extend_from_slice(&ident_buf)      // WRONG: ident first
  buf.extend_from_slice(&serial_buf)     // WRONG: serial second
  buf.extend_from_slice(body)

which means every frame the proptest harness fed to Pdu::decode had its
serial and ident fields swapped on the wire. For the random-body fuzz
target that's "probably fine" because the body bytes are garbage either
way — but for any Ok(decoded) branch that actually parses a well-formed
variant, the test would assert against a DecodedPdu whose serial and
ident had been picked up from the wrong positions. That silently masks
the exact class of regression the harness is supposed to catch
(re-ordering / size-accounting drift in the header).

## Fix

Swap the append order so the helper emits `tagged_len | serial | ident |
body`, matching decode_raw. Also swap the order that serial_buf and
ident_buf are constructed (purely cosmetic — now reads top-to-bottom in
wire order). total_payload_len stays `serial_buf.len() + ident_buf.len()
+ body.len()` — addition is commutative, but it now reads in the same
order as the wire bytes.

Update the two doc comments on the `random_header` proptest to match:
"leb128(length) leb128(serial) leb128(ident)" and the list in the
block comment above the prop also re-orders serial before ident.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
